method_atom: the study of transient dynamics of a propagating wave field as it evolves from a suddenly perturbed initial or boundary condition toward an asymptotic stationary regime, characterizing time-dependent phenomena such as buildup, decay, temporal diffraction, trapping, and propagating forerunners/front formation triggered by an abrupt change in interactions or constraints.
object_atom: quantum matter waves of massive particles, prepared in unstable initial states and subject to sudden interactions or boundary changes, now controllable, manipulable, and measurable via new experimental technology.
brief_reason: The transient-relaxation-toward-steady-state machinery (fronts, buildup, decay after a sudden perturbation) is transferable to any wave-propagation system, while the specific subject is quantum matter waves.
